headline: Agent orchestration moves to production as educators teach self-prompting loops, workflows, and durable long-running architectures.
body: The recommended channels converge on agent orchestration: Cole Medin reports frontier-lab engineers no longer prompt models directly but run orchestrator agents that spawn parallel workers, trading higher token costs for scale and requiring observability tooling [3], while IBM Technology stresses that production agents need workflows, policies, and orchestration to stay aligned with human decisions [2]. Tooling extends agent reach, with Agent-Reach pulling real internet context from Western and Chinese platforms into coding agents [1] and Cyril Imhof covering harness engineering and AI loops around Claude Fable 5 in regulated industries [6]. Supporting videos add architecture and infrastructure: Google Cloud Tech details event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation for multi-day agents [11], Hermes provides persistent memory and skill libraries across sessions [7][8], and Google's Antigravity SDK demos multi-agent social simulations [13]. On foundations, Caleb Writes Code walks through bigram language models covering tokenization, embeddings, cross entropy, softmax, logits, backpropagation, and AdamW [0], and IBM Security debates whether AI agents should autonomously manage password security amid a 200-plus CVE Patch Tuesday [5].

cited_items.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
